2604.27840.title: CastFlow: Learning Role-Specialized Agentic Workflows for Time Series Forecasting
2604.27840.paper_summary: Recently, large language models (LLMs) have shown great promise in time series forecasting. However, most existing LLM-based forecasting methods still follow a static generative paradigm that directly maps historical observations to future values in a single pass. Under this paradigm, forecasting is constrained by limited temporal pattern extraction, single-round acquisition of contextual features, one-shot forecast generation, and lack of support from ensemble forecasts. To address these limitations, in this work, we propose CastFlow, a dynamic agentic forecasting framework that enables multi-view temporal pattern extraction, multi-round contextual features acquisition, iterative forecast refinement, and forecasting with ensemble forecasts. First, CastFlow organizes the forecasting process into planning, action, forecasting, and reflection, establishing an agentic workflow. Second, this workflow is supported by a memory module that retrieves prior experience and a multi-view toolkit that constructs diagnostic evidence and provides a reliable ensemble forecast baseline. Third, CastFlow adopts a role-specialized design that combines general-purpose reasoning with specialized numerical forecasting. Under this design, a frozen LLM preserves general-purpose reasoning, while a fine-tuned domain-specific LLM performs evidence-guided numerical forecasting based on the ensemble forecast baseline, rather than from scratch. To optimize a fine-tuned domain-specific LLM, we further develop a two-stage workflow-oriented training that combines supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). To evaluate the effectiveness of CastFlow, we conduct extensive experiments on diverse datasets and show that it achieves superior overall results against strong baselines. We hope that this work can serve as a step toward more adaptive and accurate time series forecasting.
2604.27840.paper_summary_zh: 最近，大型語言模型（LLMs）在時間序列預測中顯示出巨大的潛力。<lb/>然而，大多數現有的基於LLM的預測方法仍然遵循靜態生成範式，直接將歷史觀察映射到未來值，並且僅進行一次傳遞。<lb/>在這一範式下，預測受到有限的時間模式提取、單輪上下文特徵獲取、一次性預測生成以及缺乏集成預測支持的限制。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，在本研究中，我們提出了CastFlow，一個動態代理預測框架，能夠實現多視角時間模式提取、多輪上下文特徵獲取、迭代預測精煉以及使用集成預測進行預測。<lb/>首先，CastFlow將預測過程組織為計劃、行動、預測和反思，建立了一個代理工作流程。<lb/>其次，這一工作流程由一個記憶模塊支持，該模塊檢索先前的經驗，並由一個多視角工具包構建診斷證據，提供可靠的集成預測基準。<lb/>第三，CastFlow採用角色專門化設計，將通用推理與專門的數值預測相結合。<lb/>在這一設計下，凍結的LLM保留了通用推理，而微調的特定領域LLM則基於集成預測基準執行基於證據的數值預測，而不是從頭開始。<lb/>為了優化微調的特定領域LLM，我們進一步開發了一個兩階段的工作流程導向訓練，結合了監督微調（SFT）和具有可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）。<lb/>為了評估CastFlow的有效性，我們在多樣化的數據集上進行了廣泛的實驗，顯示其在強基準下獲得了優越的整體結果。<lb/>我們希望這項工作能成為更具適應性和準確性的時間序列預測邁出的一步。
2604.27840.author: Bokai Pan et.al.
2604.27840.authors: Bokai Pan, Mingyue Cheng, Zhiding Liu, Shuo Yu, Xiaoyu Tao, Yuchong Wu, Qi Liu, Defu Lian, Enhong Chen
